title: Pungent Peruvian Gelatine Casserole
description: A low-fat, bitter and crumbly casserole that uses gelatin leaves to create a firm, flavorful base in a stone bowl.

ingredients.name: Gelatin leaves
ingredients.notes: soaked in cold water for 15 minutes

instructions: Boil the potatoes until tender, then mash them with a fork and set aside.
While the potatoes cook, soak the gelatin leaves in cold water for 15 minutes.
Drain the gelatin leaves, tear them into small pieces, and fold them into the warm broth until dissolved.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the sliced onion until translucent.
Add the crushed tomato and adobo sauce to the skillet, cooking until the mixture thickens slightly.
Stir in the cilantro, lime juice, salt, and pepper, letting the flavors meld for one minute.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let the sauce cool to room temperature.
In a dolsot stone bowl, spread the mashed potatoes evenly, creating a smooth base.
Pour the cooled sauce over the potato layer, ensuring it covers the entire surface.
Fold the soaked gelatin leaves into the sauce, mixing well to distribute the texture.
Cover the dolsot with a lid and refrigerate for at least 3 hours, allowing the gelatine to set firmly.
Before serving, garnish with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro if desired.
